messages.text: Has anyone had a look at the 2024 P3 Pure Math paper yet?
The one with the complex integration in Question 8?
Yeah, specifically the part where you have to use integration by parts twice for $e^{2x} \cos(3x)$.
I solved it last night. The trick is to treat the integral as $I$ and solve the equation for $I$ after the second iteration.
Ah, the cyclic method. Thanks, Madina! Checking my signs now.
Don't spend too much time on Pure Math today. Remember we have the IOAI prep session at 4 PM.
Are we covering the Transformers architecture or staying on CNNs?
We're moving to Attention mechanisms. Make sure you've reviewed the PyTorch documentation on `nn.MultiheadAttention`.
Quick dev question: I'm getting a hydration error in Next.js when using a custom hook for the audio player.
Is it because you're accessing `window` or `document` before the component mounts?
Probably. I'll wrap the logic in a `useEffect` hook. That should stop the server-side mismatch.
By the way, did you see the new Drizzle ORM update?
The one adding better support for relational queries? It looks so much cleaner than standard SQL joins.
Exactly. It feels like writing TypeScript rather than raw strings.
Does anyone have the PDF for the Statistics 2 textbook?
Sent it to your DM. Check the resources folder too.
Back to AI—has everyone finished the data preprocessing task?
Still cleaning the CSV. There are so many null values in the 'labels' column.
Try using Pandas `interpolate()` or just drop them if they're less than 5% of the total dataset.
Dropping them is safer for this specific Olympiad task. We need high-quality data over quantity here.
Are we still meeting at the library or just doing a Google Meet?
Library is better for the Physics experiment review. We need the actual equipment.
Agreed. I need help with the pendulum timing errors.
I'll be there by 4:15 PM. Bring your laptops fully charged.
Will do. I'll bring the extension cord just in case.
Has anyone looked into the UI for the course platform?
I'm keeping it minimalist. Dark mode by default, similar to the Spotify layout.
Make sure the sidebar is collapsible. Screen space is vital for code snippets.
Good point. I'll use Framer Motion for a smooth transition.
Does anyone want to grab coffee before the session?
I'm in! Meet at the usual spot near the entrance?
Count me in too. I need the caffeine to survive the Statistics review.
Don't be late. We have a lot of ground to cover for the Olympiad city stage.
We won't be. Aziz, did you fix the bug in the repo?
Yeah, pushed it 10 minutes ago. It was a simple typo in the `.env` file.
Classic. Okay, see you all in a bit.
Wait, bring the printed Physics formulas too!
Got them. Printed and stapled.
Awesome. Loading the car now.
Actually, one last thing: bring the SAT prep book if you have it.
I'll bring mine. Aziz has the digital version.
Yep, already on my tablet. See ya!
